title: Group X - Total Body Strength and Conditioning
description:
Instructor-led strength and conditioning class designed to hit every major muscle group in just 30 minutes. Using a combination of compound lifts, bodyweight movements, and targeted conditioning work, each session is programmed to build real strength and leave you energized for the rest of the day. Instructors scale every movement so you work at the right intensity for your level. 
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Registration is not required. 
 FREE for students with a valid Montclair State ID 
 
 Where and When? 
 
 Thursdays, June 1st - June 25th 
 12:45 pm - 1:15 pm 
 Student Recreation Center - Multipurpose Room 1 
 
 Instructor: Alexander

title: Bearfort Mountain (Advanced)
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
Join Outdoor Adventure for one of the most strenuous but spectacular hikes of the New Jersey and New York area. As we hike the Bearfort Mountain ridge past the Greenwood Lake, rhododendron tunnel, surprise lake, and west pond. Sure to be a breathtaking view the whole way up to the summit of the mountain.  
 How do I sign-up? 
 
 Register online through  Fusion , in the Student Recreation Center main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students $10;  $20 Faculty/Staff 
 Registration Opens on Fusion on Tuesday, May 26, 2026 
 
 Where and when? 
 
 June 29, 2026 
 10:00am –5:00pm 
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
 What to bring:  Water, lunch, weather appropriate clothing, boots ( sneakers not acceptable ), lunch, snacks, and sunscreen. 
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures.

title: Red Hawk Pantry
description:
1) Please have your Event Pass, Student ID, CWID and have a  PantrySoft Registration .  2) Please bring your own shopping bag! ***Visitors are allowed to come once/week*** 
 Our Mission:   We aim to address food insecurity across Montclair State University. The Red Hawk Pantry is open to all students, staff, and faculty at no cost.  Stay updated by following us on Instagram:  @montclair_redhawkpantry 
 🌻 Summer 2026 Hours 
 
 Wednesdays: 12-2pm 
 
 If you are unable to visit during our regular hours, please reach out via email at redhawkpantry@montclair.edu

title: Prestigious Scholarships Info Session
description: Join our weekly Prestigious Scholarships Info Sessions to learn about nationally competitive scholarships and fellowships that support graduate study, research, internships, public service, and international opportunities. Learn more about awards such as Marshall Rhodes, Truman, and more, while exploring eligibility, application timelines, and program support at  Montclair State University . Open to students from all majors. Sessions are held Wednesdays at 1:00 PM on Zoom. RSVP on Engage to receive the Zoom link. A google calendar invite will be sent once RSVP.

title: Group X - Yoga
description:
Yoga is a practice for the mind, body, and spirit. In this class, participants will move through a variety of seated and standing poses, connecting with their breath and engaging in strengthening, flexibility, and mindfulness–with options for all levels of experience. 
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Registration is not required. 
 FREE for students with a valid Montclair State ID 
 
 Where and When? 
 
 Mondays, July 6 - July 30 
 12:15 - 1:00pm 
 Student Recreation Center - Multipurpose Room 1 
 
 Instructor: Annabelle

title: Camping and Surfing Trip
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
July 7th, 8th and 9th 
 $30 Student; $75 Faculty/Staff 
 Join Campus Recreation for a new and exciting trip where we will camp in the beautiful Wharton State Forest and surf on the beaches of Ocean City, NJ! We will head to Wharton State Forest for a relaxing night of camping. The next day we will head to Ocean City to enjoy the sun and waves. We return to the campsite that evening, and depart Thursday morning to come back to campus. We provide all of the equipment and transportation, including tents, sleeping bags, ground pads, surfing lessons and surfboards, plus beach badges for the afternoon! Meals are included. 
     How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Rec Center main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students $30; Faculty/Staff $75 
 
   Where and When? 
 
 July 7th, 8th and 9th, 2026 
 July 7th 10 am- July 9th 5pm  
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
 What to bring:  Water bottle, bug spray, sunscreen, towel, bathing suit, clothes for 2 nights and 3 days, toiletries, chargers, bag for wet clothing, sneakers, money (optional for boardwalk) 
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites, falls from a height, abrasions from sand, uncontrolled interactions with wildlife, drowning from rough surf.

title: Hike - Ramapo Mountain
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
This trail features a loop around the beautiful Ramapo Lake into a bit of steep climb to the ruins of the Van Slyke Castle and water tower. From the water tower, there is a view gorgeous view of NYC. 
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students  $10 ; Faculty/Staff $20 
 
 Where and when? 
 
 Thursday, July 16th, 2026 
 10:00am – 5:00pm 
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
 What to bring:  Water, lunch, weather appropriate clothing, boots/ sneakers ,  lunch, snacks, and sunscreen.

title: Whitewater Rafting Trip
location: Meet at the Student Recreation Center
description:
Coordinating with the release of the dam in the Lehigh River, this whitewater rafting trip is sure to be a blast. Perfect for all skill levels. This is a great way to GET YOUR FEET WET!  
   
 How do I Sign-up? 
 
 Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students  $20 ; Faculty/Staff $60 
 
   
 Where and when? 
 
 Saturday, July 25th, 2026 
 7:00am – 7:00pm 
 Begins at Student Recreation Center 
 
   
 What to bring:  Lunch, water, snacks, towel, a change of clothes and sandals with a heel strap, old sneakers, or water shoes. Flip-flops or slides are not allowed. For warmer days, wear a swim suit or quick-drying shorts/shirt; for cooler days, bring extra layers, but no cotton. Wetsuits are included for those that want them. Half/full wetsuits can be rented from the organization. Half is $6, full is $12, jacket is $4.  
   
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, limited vision, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures.

title: Free Summer Membership for Faculty/Staff
description:
Free summer memberships at the Student Recreation Center are back and better than ever! This year, we've extended the program to three full months (May 7 – August 7) and have newly upgraded fitness spaces and equipment for you to enjoy. Membership provides unlimited access to our state-of-the-art facilities, indoor heated pool, and more. No registration required, simply bring your employee ID to access the building all summer long. 
 
 
 
 
 How do I sign-up? 
 
 Registration not required 
 For employees of Montclair/Bloomfield 
 
 Where and when? 
 
 Whenever SRC is open, from  May 7th - August 7th  
 Student Recreation Center

description: Join our weekly Prestigious Scholarships Info Sessions to learn about nationally competitive scholarships and fellowships that support graduate study, research, internships, public service, and international opportunities. Learn more about awards such as Marshall Rhodes, Truman, and more, while exploring eligibility, application timelines, and program support at  Montclair State University . Open to students from all majors. Sessions are held Wednesdays at 1:00 PM on Zoom. RSVP on Engage to receive the Zoom link. A google calendar invite will be sent once RSVP.

title: Study Abroad Fair Fall 2026
description:
Ready to take your education global? Join us at the Study Abroad Fair and explore a world of opportunities waiting just beyond your campus. Discover programs around the world, meet representatives from our partners and providers, and hear firsthand stories from students who’ve already taken the leap. 
 Whether you're dreaming of practicing your language skills, expanding your horizons in a new corner of the globe, or even seeing a different part of the United States, this is your chance to find the perfect fit. Learn about scholarships, internships, and how to make your journey abroad both affordable and unforgettable. 
 📅 Date: Wednesday, September 9, 2026 📍 Location: Student Center Ballrooms ⏰ Time: 1:30-3:30pm

title: AEW: MAJOR KICKOFF 2026
location: The Student Center Quad & Ballroom A
description:
Major Kickoff a high-energy, campus-wide event that transforms a traditional majors fair into an interactive and dynamic experience that sets the tone for major exploration.  Major Kickoff  serves as a central hub where exploratory ("undeclared") students can dive into the wide array of majors offered at Montclair in a fun and engaging way. Students will engage with peers, faculty, and staff; discover pathways to get involved in various programs; and gain the guidance they need to make confident, informed decisions about their major. With the opportunity to declare a major on the spot,  Major Kickoff  empowers students to take charge of their academic journey. 
 With over 50 departments, programs, clubs, and more on site, you never know what you might discover or who you might meet.  Enjoy free food, games, music, and giveaways, win prizes and T-shirts , and maybe even discover your major. 
 Major Kickoff is part of Academic Exploration Week, a longstanding immersive experience presented by The Discovery Program. To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: AEW: FIND YOUR CENTER 2026
location: Student Center Ballrooms ABC / The Quad
description:
The Discovery Program is just one of the many different departments on campus committed to your success at Montclair State University. 
 Join us and our campus partners for an engaging and centering experience to help you explore all that Montclair has to offer. Use the clues at check-in to help you figure out what each center has to offer you.   Come have fun, make connections, enjoy some refreshments and snacks, and learn about the various centers on campus! 
 Find Your Center is part of  Academic Exploration Week , a longstanding immersive experience presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: AEW: MAJOR MYSTERY 2026
description:
Major Mystery : Can You Crack the Case? Strange clues… puzzling paths… and majors shrouded in mystery. Gather your team, study the evidence, and connect the right majors to the right careers before time runs out! Solve career “cases” by piecing together path cards filled with cryptic clues. The more connections you crack, the more points you earn. Will your group uncover the truth… or will the mystery remain unsolved? 
 Major Mystery is part of  Academic Exploration Week , a longstanding immersive experience presented by  The Discovery Program . To contact the Discovery Program, email us at  discoveryprogram@montclair.edu , or visit us at  montclair.edu/discovery-program .

title: CCOM Alumni Panel
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
location: Presentation Hall - College of Communication and Media
description:
Join alumni from the  College of Communication and Media  as they share their career journeys, discuss how they transitioned from college to the professional world, and offer advice for students interested in creative careers. 
 Panelists will discuss : 
 
 Building a career in the communication and media industries 
 Navigating internships, auditions, and professional opportunities 
 Developing industry connections and professional networks 
 Skills that helped them succeed after graduation 
 Challenges, opportunities, and lessons learned along the way 
 
 Featured career areas include : 
 
 Assistant Account Executive 
 Co-Founder & Creative Director 
 Social Video Producer 
 Video Editor & Productions 
 
 Students will have the opportunity to ask questions, learn about different career paths, and gain firsthand insights from CCOM alumni who have successfully launched their careers. 
   
 Register on Handshake here!
